context_awareness.persistence_mechanism: ContextStore (in-memory stub; must implement save() and list_for_user())

completed: Test infrastructure (base tests passing, validation/unit tests written)

apply_context() logic (tests written, stub returns base_answer unchanged)

questions: What does 'use context to influence answer' mean operationally?
How should context be persisted (in-memory, file, database)?
Which context types are MVP vs. nice-to-have?
How should apply_context() modify answers (rule-based, template-based, LLM-based)?

verify.success_criteria: All test layers passing (base, validation, unit)

next_recommended.command: gentle-ai sdd explore --change='CAG context augmentation feature'
next_recommended.rationale: Clarify context incorporation strategies and persistence options before proceeding to design and implementation
next_recommended.do_not_skip: Do NOT proceed to /sdd-apply or implementation until /sdd-explore → /sdd-spec → /sdd-design are complete. This ensures implementation aligns with intent.

summary: SDD initialization complete for Python 3.14.6 pure-stdlib CAG system with strict TDD discipline. openspec/ artifacts created with testing capabilities, stack detection, and SDD phase references. Test layers identified: base (3/3 ✅), validation (0/3 ❌ expected), unit (3/11 ⚠️ TDD red). Strict TDD auto-detected true from unittest runner + passing base tests. Engram memory persisted (3 observations, session summary). Ready for /sdd-explore phase to clarify feature requirements before proceeding to implementation.
